attempts.strategy: Reproduce iter-131 body's Classical.choose-chain on Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega; introduce Γ(Spec k, U)-algebra structure on Γ(G.left, V) and Γ(G.left, V)-algebra structure on k; show goal as TensorProduct form (via ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'); apply Module.finrank_baseChange to push finrank through; finish with Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank.
attempts.code_tried:
let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
  set e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) := (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]; exact hxV
  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k := ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
  show Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V)) (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
attempts.lean_error: warning: The `show` tactic should only be used to indicate intermediate goal states for readability. However, this tactic invocation changed the goal.
attempts.goal_after: no goals (after rw + exact); the `show` warning indicated a soft style issue but proof closed cleanly
attempts.insight: The Classical.choose-chain extraction from the body of cotangentSpaceAtIdentity definitionally exposes the underlying TensorProduct carrier via ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'. `Nontrivial Γ(G.left,V)` is discharged via `RingHom.domain_nontrivial` (the ring map into k is into a field, so the domain is nontrivial); this discharges the StrongRankCondition instance hypothesis of Module.finrank_baseChange (via Mathlib's `commRing_strongRankCondition` instance). The `show` warning indicated the tactic was changing the goal, not just exposing the same goal — the right fix was to use `change` instead of `show`.

attempts.strategy: Style cleanup: swap `show` for `change` to silence the Lean linter warning. Both tactics produce a definitional-equality change of the goal; `change` is the correct primitive when the goal is genuinely changing carrier-shape via a defeq reduction.
attempts.code_tried: change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
attempts.goal_after: Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
attempts.insight: `change` is the right tactic when shifting the goal's carrier to a definitionally-equal form (here ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj' delta-reduces to the TensorProduct). `show` is reserved for restating the goal verbatim to aid readability; the Lean linter warns when `show` is being used to actually shift the goal.
